title: obInit writes one extra resource_lookup_data_array entry (index file_entry_max-1) that retail leaves untouched

evidence.path: src/game/ob.c:168-175 vs GLOBAL_ASM obInit 0x7F0BCC28 (glabel ob.c:185); retail loop 0x7F0BCC80-0x7F0BCCA8 with end bound register a1 = resource_lookup_data_array + (file_entry_max-1)*20 computed 0x7F0BCC64-0x7F0BCC7C

history.evidence: src/game/ob.c:168-175 vs GLOBAL_ASM obInit 0x7F0BCC28 (glabel ob.c:185); retail loop 0x7F0BCC80-0x7F0BCCA8 with end bound register a1 = resource_lookup_data_array + (file_entry_max-1)*20 computed 0x7F0BCC64-0x7F0BCC7C
history.note: Retail obInit initializes resource_lookup_data_array via an ASCENDING pointer loop: v0 walks from array index 1 (0x7F0BCC7C sets v0 = base + 0x14) upward by stride 0x14, storing rom_size = frt-next.hw_address minus frt-cur.hw_address at offset 0, then zero to poolRemaining 0x4, pc_size 0x8, rom_remaining 0xC; it continues while the post-incremented pointer is strictly less than a1 = base + (file_entry_max-1)*20. That bound stops after writing index file_entry_max-2, so retail never touches index file_entry_max-1. The NATIVE_PORT C at ob.c:168 instead uses a DESCENDING loop for i = file_entry_max-1 down to 1, which DOES write index file_entry_max-1 (rom_size = frt[file_entry_max].hw - frt[file_entry_max-1].hw, and the three zero fields). Net divergence: the port initializes one extra entry (the last file index) that retail leaves at its BSS/prior value. Order of writes is irrelevant (independent per-entry stores); the only behavioral difference is that extra entry. Likely benign if index file_entry_max-1 is an unused end sentinel, but if that entry is ever consumed by the OB file loader its rom_size differs (port computed vs retail zero). Needs an oracle over the ob file-resource table to confirm whether index file_entry_max-1 is loaded before deciding fix (default-on) vs documented parity.
